summary:
The apically repositioned flap may include preserving attached gingiva by carefully managing splits during surgery; thus, its impact varies based on specific application details. This highlights potential benefits when using such flaps judiciously near wide areas with sufficient keratinized tissue.
To summarize Agent2’s reasoning:

The apically repositioned flap often involves strategies to preserve attached gingiva through careful surgical management, making its effect variable depending on implementation specifics. By selectively handling splits, surgeons can maintain or enhance soft-tissue integrity around treated sites where adequate keratinized gum exists. Summary ends here. To ensure clarity, no additional text will follow.
